text:
(also known as ) Neon Genesis Evangelion Sega Saturn Perfect Guide p.143 is an Angel that is original to the Neon Genesis Evangelion: Second Impression video game.

Profile

The design concept seems to take what Ritsuko Akagi said about Sandalphon's "chrysalis" to heart, by having the entity go through larval and pupal (chrysalis) stages before reaching adulthood ("imago"). As a chrysalis, it can teleport short distances and in its true form can levitate, extend the three spikes from each spinning wheel-like structure on its sides into a blade, and fire green energy balls from it's body.

It has no known name, although its Bandai Carddass cards refer to it as "Insubstantial Angel". Because its Core is contained within the body of Mayumi Yamagishi, the Angel is forced to leave during battle with the Evangelion units to give Shinji Ikari and her time to think about what to do.

There are three different stages:
Larva
In its first form is a giant floating larva. From its lower part several thin legs emerge. In this state, the angel can teleport at short distances. Its body structure is divided into several disc-shaped parts, and parts of its body are turned into weapons that it throws to attack. Also, when attacked by an enemy, it scatters to avoid them, then gathers back together and returns to its original form. Evangelion Chronicle 04 p.26 

Pupa
In many lepidoptera, the pupa is called chrysalis. After spending some time in its floating larval state, the countless spikes on its body grow all at once, and the tips spread out to form a cocoon. Evangelion Chronicle 04 p.26 the angel forms a gigantic chrysalis embedded between buildings. After staying a brief period of time, it reaches adulthood and assumes it's true form. 

Imago
In its Imago form, the angel is a gigantic floating creature, whose appearance is vaguely resembling an insect. It has a thin and branched body in an exotic way, without limbs.

From its upper part arise two structures similar to a pair of wings. On both sides of its body it has revolving structures similar to wheels, from which three peaks emerge. It attacks with its elastic spikes, absorbs EVA energy, and has the power to cut through A.T. Fields. The attacking Angel is like shadows with no substance, and revive even if they are defeated. Evangelion Chronicle 04 p.26 

History

Neon Genesis Evangelion: Second Impression

Sometime after Ireul's appearance , the Insubstantial Angel appears as a larva, floating on the outskirts of Tokyo-3. After a first attempt to eliminate it with conventional weapons, NERV sends Evangelion Unit-01 with Evangelion Unit-02 or Evangelion Unit-00 (depending on the player's choice). As the fight takes place, Shinji Ikari spots a civilian, Mayumi Yamagishi, right next to the place where the fight takes place. After several minutes of combat, the Angel is defeated and explodes. However, a few days later, it returns this time in the form of a Chrysalis. NERV sends out Evangelions again, but in the face of the enemy's inactivity, they prefers to wait to see how things develops. At dusk, the Angel finally emerges from the chrysalis in its final form, Imago. The fight begins and continues until night falls. Finally, after several hours, the Angel is finally defeated.

Trivia

The Angel was designed by Mahiro Maeda. Neon Genesis Evangelion Sega Saturn Perfect Guide p.143
